subject: [PATCH v4 1/3] cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c

reviews.author: Alison Schofield
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM, and questioned whether the intention is to conditionally compile it based on this configuration option.
reviews.raw_body:
This always adds region_pmem.o regardless of CONFIG_CXL_PMEM.
Are you heading towards conditionally compiling, where region_pmem.o is
omitted if !CONFIG_CXL_PMEM? 

Similar question for region_dax.o

--Alison

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges reviewer's concern about adding more Kconfig cruft, but doesn't commit to a fix; instead suggests that the current approach might be acceptable despite potential bloat for some deployments.
reviews.raw_body:
I hadn't considered going that far, but it doesn't seem unreasonable?

It just seemed natural to break out the code this way, but I don't know
if adding more Kconfig cruft is worth it just to save some .o's and a
smaller amount of code.

The recent pmem/nvdimm Kconfig change does kind of show that we might
have some bloat for some deployments which might not care about pmem -
for example.

~Gregory

2026-03-22.patch_summary: This patch moves the logic for managing persistent memory (pmem) regions from the overloaded region.c file into a new, dedicated region_pmem.c file. This separation makes it clear that this code only applies to pmem regions and helps to declutter the original file. No functional changes are made as part of this refactoring effort.
